name: PSID-8
description: Annotation schema for the PSID-8 benchmark: 8 physical-security incident classes with frame-level bounding boxes and compositional attributes (object, action, environment, context). Vocabularies are imported verbatim from the executable CVAT label configuration (attributes.json) validated in the annotation pilot.
annotation_unit: bounding box per frame, linked to an event_id persistent across the clip
cvat_export_format: COCO 1.0 (instances_default.json). NEVER export as YOLO from CVAT: attributes would be lost. Convert with psid8/scripts/coco_to_yolo.py, which writes YOLO labels plus .attrs.json sidecars.

classes.name: accident
classes.operational_definition: Unintentional collision or impact involving vehicle(s) and/or person(s), from first contact until the scene stabilizes.

classes.name: suspicious_behavior
classes.operational_definition: Movement pattern inconsistent with normal use of the site: loitering (>=2 passes by the same point), prolonged observation of access points, testing doors/windows, following a person.

classes.name: crime
classes.operational_definition: Physical assault, robbery, theft in progress, or threat with a visible weapon, from the start of the hostile action until the parties separate.

classes.name: fire
classes.operational_definition: Visible uncontrolled flame or smoke. Annotate the visible flame/smoke region, not the presumed source.

classes.name: intrusion
classes.operational_definition: Person crossing a perimeter/restricted zone defined in the scenario metadata, in any posture (standing, crouching, crawling).

classes.name: suspicious_object
classes.operational_definition: Portable object that remains without a visible carrier for >= T_abandon (default 30 s; recorded per scenario) in a circulation area.

classes.name: fall
classes.operational_definition: Unintentional transition from an upright/seated posture to the ground. Annotate from loss of balance until rest on the ground. A person already lying down WITHOUT an observed transition is NOT a fall.

classes.name: vandalism
classes.operational_definition: Intentional property damage: graffiti in progress, breaking glass/equipment, damage to a third party's vehicle.

context.desc: condition that makes the event an incident

negatives_policy: Each scenario must contain negative clips (normal operation) at a ratio >= 1:1 with positive clips, including documented hard negatives (e.g., person lying down without a fall; barbecue smoke; sports running).

quality_gates.adjudication: a third annotator resolves disagreements; decisions are logged
